ucs.title: [LLM] Darkhotel (DUBNIUM/Zigzag Hail) fake-software-update dropper from hotel/captive-portal staging path
ucs.description: Hunts the Darkhotel signature pattern of a digitally-signed binary masquerading as a legitimate updater (Flash, GoogleToolbar, Adobe, Realtek) that lands in a user-writable path after a hotel/Wi-Fi drive-by (T1189) and either sleeps to defeat sandboxes (T1497) or pulls a second-stage via PowerShell/cmd (T1059). Darkhotel famously hijacks hotel networks to inject these masqueraded updates against traveling executives, then signs payloads with stolen/forged certificates (T1553.002).
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parent values(Processes.process_path) as path values(Processes.process_hash) as sha256 from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.user!="*SYSTEM" Processes.user!="*$" (Processes.process_name IN ("*update*.exe","*setup*.exe","*flash*.exe","*adobe*.exe","*googletoolbar*.exe","*hwp*.exe","*realtek*.exe")) (Processes.process_path="*\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\AppData\\Roaming\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\Downloads\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\Public\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\ProgramData\\*") by host Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.process_path Processes.process_hash Processes.parent_process_name | rename Processes.* as * | join type=outer host process_hash [| tstats summariesonly=true count from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name IN ("*update*.exe","*setup*.exe","*flash*.exe","*adobe*.exe","*googletoolbar*.exe","*hwp*.exe","*realtek*.exe") (Processes.process_name IN ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","mshta.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","bitsadmin.exe","certutil.exe")) by host Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_name Processes.process | rename Processes.parent_process_name as process_name Processes.process_name as child_process Processes.process as child_cmd] | where isnotnull(child_process) | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime) | table firstTime host user process_name path process_hash parent child_process child_cmd
ucs.kql:
let LookbackDays = 7d;
let UpdaterNames = dynamic(["update","setup","flash","adobe","googletoolbar","hwp","realtek","java","silverlight"]);
let UserPaths = dynamic([@"\AppData\Local\Temp\", @"\AppData\Roaming\", @"\Downloads\", @"\Public\", @"\ProgramData\"]);
let LolBins = dynamic(["powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","mshta.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","bitsadmin.exe","certutil.exe","curl.exe"]);
let Droppers = DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where AccountName !endswith "$"
    | where FolderPath has_any (UserPaths)
    | where FileName has_any (UpdaterNames)
    | where FileName endswith ".exe"
    // Darkhotel relocates signed binaries OR drops new ones whose version-info signer doesn't match Microsoft
    | where InitiatingProcessVersionInfoCompanyName !has "Microsoft"
    | project DropperTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName,
              DropperFile = FileName, DropperPath = FolderPath,
              DropperSha256 = SHA256, DropperPid = ProcessId,
              DropperCompany = ProcessVersionInfoCompanyName,
              DropperOriginalName = ProcessVersionInfoOriginalFileName,
              DropperCmd = ProcessCommandLine;
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
| where FileName in~ (LolBins)
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any ("http", "DownloadString", "IEX", "-enc", "-EncodedCommand", "FromBase64String", "WebClient", "BitsTransfer", "-w hidden", "-windowstyle hidden")
   or InitiatingProcessFileName has_any (UpdaterNames)
| join kind=inner Droppers on $left.InitiatingProcessId == $right.DropperPid, DeviceId
| where Timestamp between (DropperTime .. DropperTime + 10m)
| project DropperTime, ChildTime = Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName,
          DropperFile, DropperPath, DropperSha256, DropperCompany, DropperOriginalName,
          ChildProcess = FileName, ChildCmd = ProcessCommandLine,
          DelaySec = datetime_diff('second', Timestamp, DropperTime)
| order by DropperTime desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let LookbackDays = 7d;
let UpdaterPattern = @"(?i)\\(update|setup|flash|adobe|googletoolbar|hwp|realtek|java|silverlight)[^\\]*\.exe$";
let UserPathPattern = @"(?i)\\(AppData\\Local\\Temp|AppData\\Roaming|Downloads|Public|ProgramData)\\";
let LolBins = dynamic(["powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","mshta.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","bitsadmin.exe","certutil.exe","curl.exe"]);
let Droppers = SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where EventID == 4688
    | where Account !endswith "$"
    | where NewProcessName matches regex UserPathPattern
    | where NewProcessName matches regex UpdaterPattern
    | project DropperTime = TimeGenerated, Computer, Account,
              DropperImage = NewProcessName, DropperPid = NewProcessId,
              DropperCmd = CommandLine;
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
| where EventID == 4688
| extend ChildName = tostring(split(NewProcessName, "\\")[-1])
| where tolower(ChildName) in (LolBins)
| where CommandLine has_any ("http", "DownloadString", "IEX", "-enc", "-EncodedCommand", "FromBase64String", "WebClient", "BitsTransfer", "-w hidden", "-windowstyle hidden")
| join kind=inner Droppers on Computer, $left.ProcessId == $right.DropperPid
| where TimeGenerated between (DropperTime .. DropperTime + 10m)
| project DropperTime, ChildTime = TimeGenerated, Computer, Account,
          DropperImage, DropperCmd,
          ChildImage = NewProcessName, ChildCmd = CommandLine,
          DelaySec = datetime_diff('second', TimeGenerated, DropperTime)
| order by DropperTime desc
ucs.rationale: Darkhotel's distinctive delivery is a fake software-update binary planted via hotel Wi-Fi/captive-portal injection, and the dropper is almost always named after legit updaters (Flash/Adobe/Realtek/GoogleToolbar/HWP) — a pattern documented by Kaspersky and Microsoft. Generic 'update.exe in Temp' alone is noisy, but the actor-specific combination — masqueraded updater name + user-writable path + lol

ucs.title: [LLM] Darkhotel spearphish: Office/HWP decoy spawning binary masquerading as Windows system file with HKCU Run-key persistence
ucs.description: Catches Darkhotel's spearphishing chain (T1566.001 → T1203/T1204.002 → T1547.001) where a decoy document — frequently a Korean Hancom HWP, RTF, or weaponised Office file — exploits/spawns a payload that masquerades as a legitimate Windows binary (svchost.exe, igfxtray.exe, conhost.exe, wuauclt.exe, ctfmon.exe) but executes from a user profile directory, then writes a HKCU Run / RunOnce value within minutes for persistence. Darkhotel is documented to disguise loaders with these exact filenames and rely on user-writable persistence to survive without admin rights.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as spawnTime values(Processes.process) as childCmd values(Processes.process_path) as childPath values(Processes.process_hash) as childHash values(Processes.process_id) as childPid from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name IN ("winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","outlook.exe","hwp.exe","hword.exe","acrord32.exe","acrobat.exe","eqnedt32.exe") Processes.user!="*$" Processes.process_name IN ("svchost.exe","igfxtray.exe","conhost.exe","wuauclt.exe","ctfmon.exe","taskhost.exe","taskhostw.exe","explorer.exe","lsass.exe","services.exe","winlogon.exe","smss.exe") (Processes.process_path!="C:\\Windows\\System32\\*" Processes.process_path!="C:\\Windows\\SysWOW64\\*" Processes.process_path!="C:\\Windows\\WinSxS\\*" Processes.process_path!="C:\\Program Files*") by host Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_name Processes.process_path Processes.process_hash Processes.process_id | rename Processes.* as * | join type=inner host [| tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as regTime values(Registry.registry_key_name) as regKey values(Registry.registry_value_name) as regValueName values(Registry.registry_value_data) as regValueData values(Registry.process_id) as regPid from datamodel=Endpoint.Registry where Registry.registry_path="*\\CurrentVersion\\Run*" Registry.action="modified" by host Registry.process_id | rename Registry.* as *] | where regPid==process_id OR (regTime>=spawnTime AND regTime<=spawnTime+600) | convert ctime(spawnTime) ctime(regTime) | table spawnTime regTime host user parent_process_name process_name process_path process_hash regKey regValueName regValueData
ucs.kql:
let LookbackDays = 7d;
let PersistenceWindow = 15m;
let OfficeDecoyParents = dynamic(["winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","outlook.exe","hwp.exe","hword.exe","hcell.exe","hshow.exe","acrord32.exe","acrobat.exe","eqnedt32.exe"]);
let MasqueradeNames = dynamic(["svchost.exe","igfxtray.exe","conhost.exe","wuauclt.exe","ctfmon.exe","taskhost.exe","taskhostw.exe","explorer.exe","lsass.exe","services.exe","winlogon.exe","smss.exe","spoolsv.exe"]);
let MasqueradedSpawns = DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where AccountName !endswith "$"
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (OfficeDecoyParents)
    | where FileName in~ (MasqueradeNames)
    // genuine Windows binaries live under System32/SysWOW64/WinSxS — anywhere else is masquerading
    | where not(FolderPath startswith @"C:\Windows\System32\")
    | where not(FolderPath startswith @"C:\Windows\SysWOW64\")
    | where not(FolderPath startswith @"C:\Windows\WinSxS\")
    | where not(FolderPath startswith @"C:\Program Files\")
    | where not(FolderPath startswith @"C:\Program Files (x86)\")
    | project SpawnTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName,
              ParentDoc = InitiatingProcessFileName,
              ParentDocCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
              MasqFile = FileName, MasqPath = FolderPath,
              MasqSha256 = SHA256, MasqPid = ProcessId,
              MasqOriginalName = ProcessVersionInfoOriginalFileName,
              MasqCompany = ProcessVersionInfoCompanyName;
DeviceRegistryEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
| where ActionType in ("RegistryValueSet","RegistryKeyCreated")
| where RegistryKey has_any (@"\CurrentVersion\Run", @"\CurrentVersion\RunOnce", @"\Explorer\Run", @"\Userinit")
| join kind=inner MasqueradedSpawns on DeviceId
| where Timestamp between (SpawnTime .. SpawnTime + PersistenceWindow)
| project SpawnTime, PersistenceTime = Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName,
          ParentDoc, MasqFile, MasqPath, MasqSha256, MasqOriginalName, MasqCompany,
          RegistryKey, RegistryValueName, RegistryValueData,
          PersistDelaySec = datetime_diff('second', Timestamp, SpawnTime)
| order by SpawnTime desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let LookbackDays = 7d;
let PersistenceWindow = 15m;
let OfficeDecoyParents = dynamic(["winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","outlook.exe","hwp.exe","hword.exe","hcell.exe","hshow.exe","acrord32.exe","acrobat.exe","eqnedt32.exe"]);
let MasqueradeNames = dynamic(["svchost.exe","igfxtray.exe","conhost.exe","wuauclt.exe","ctfmon.exe","taskhost.exe","taskhostw.exe","explorer.exe","lsass.exe","services.exe","winlogon.exe","smss.exe","spoolsv.exe"]);
let MasqueradedSpawns = SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where EventID == 4688
    | where Account !endswith "$"
    | extend ParentName = tolower(tostring(split(ParentProcessName, "\\")[-1])),
             ChildName  = tolower(tostring(split(NewProcessName, "\\")[-1]))
    | where ParentName in (OfficeDecoyParents)
    | where ChildName  in (MasqueradeNames)
    | where not(NewProcessName startswith @"C:\Windows\System32\")
    | where not(NewProcessName startswith @"C:\Windows\SysWOW64\")
    | where not(NewProcessName startswith @"C:\Windows\WinSxS\")
    | where not(NewProcessName startswith @"C:\Program Files\")
    | where not(NewProcessName startswith @"C:\Program Files (x86)\")
    | project SpawnTime = TimeGenerated, Computer, Account,
              ParentDoc = ParentName, MasqImage = NewProcessName,
              MasqCmd = CommandLine, MasqPid = NewProcessId;
let RunKeyWrites = ImRegistryEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where EventType in ("RegistryValueSet","RegistryKeyCreated")
    | where RegistryKey has_any (@"\CurrentVersion\Run", @"\CurrentVersion\RunOnce", @"\Explorer\Run", @"\Userinit")
    | project RegTime = TimeGenerated, DvcHostname, RegistryKey, RegistryValue, RegistryValueData, ActingProcessName;
MasqueradedSpawns
| join kind=inner RunKeyWrites on $left.Computer == $right.DvcHostname
| where RegTime between (SpawnTime .. SpawnTime + PersistenceWindow)
| project SpawnTime, RegTime, Computer, Account,
          ParentDoc, MasqImage, MasqCmd,
          RegistryKey, RegistryValue, RegistryValueData, ActingProcessName,
          PersistDelaySec = datetime_diff('second', RegTime, SpawnTime)
| order by SpawnTime desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Darkhotel - System-Binary Masquerade Spawned by Office/HWP Decoy
id: 8a1d3c12-5b9e-4f1a-9c33-darkhotel-masq01
status: experimental
description: Detects an executable named like a legitimate Windows system binary (svchost.exe, igfxtray.exe, etc.) running outside System32/SysWOW64/Program Files, spawned by an Office or Hancom HWP process. Aligned with Darkhotel (G0012) loader masquerading.
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0012/
  - https://securelist.com/the-darkhotel-apt/66779/
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/04
tags:
  - attack.initial_access
  - attack.execution
  - attack.defense_evasion
  - attack.t1036.005
  - attack.t1203
  - attack.t1566.001
  - attack.g0012
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_parent:
    ParentImage|endswith:
      - '\winword.exe'
      - '\excel.exe'
      - '\powerpnt.exe'
      - '\outlook.exe'
      - '\hwp.exe'
      - '\hword.exe'
      - '\hcell.exe'
      - '\hshow.exe'
      - '\acrord32.exe'
      - '\acrobat.exe'
      - '\eqnedt32.exe'
  selection_child_name:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\svchost.exe'
      - '\igfxtray.exe'
      - '\conhost.exe'
      - '\wuauclt.exe'
      - '\ctfmon.exe'
      - '\taskhost.exe'
      - '\taskhostw.exe'
      - '\explorer.exe'
      - '\lsass.exe'
      - '\services.exe'
      - '\winlogon.exe'
      - '\smss.exe'
      - '\spoolsv.exe'
  filter_legit_paths:
    Image|startswith:
      - 'C:\Windows\System32\'
      - 'C:\Windows\SysWOW64\'
      - 'C:\Windows\WinSxS\'
      - 'C:\Program Files\'
      - 'C:\Program Files (x86)\'
  condition: selection_parent and selection_child_name and not filter_legit_paths
falsepositives:
  - Some legacy installers drop helper binaries with these names; tune by image hash signer.
level: high
ucs.rationale: Darkhotel — flagged as DUBNIUM by Microsoft and Zigzag Hail by Mandiant — has been observed delivering loaders via Korean Hancom HWP attachments and weaponised Office/PDF docs (T1566.001 + T1203), then writing a renamed payload to user-writable directories that mimics a Windows system binary (T1036.005) and chaining HKCU\...\Run for low-privilege persistence (T1547.001). The combined signal — Offi
_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma rule identifier must be an UUID
